2604.07123.title: Language Bias under Conflicting Information in Multilingual LLMs
2604.07123.paper_summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) have been shown to contain biases in the process of integrating conflicting information when answering questions. Here we ask whether such biases also exist with respect to which language is used for each conflicting piece of information. To answer this question, we extend the conflicting needles in a haystack paradigm to a multilingual setting and perform a comprehensive set of evaluations with naturalistic news domain data in five different languages, for a range of multilingual LLMs of different sizes. We find that all LLMs tested, including GPT-5.2, ignore the conflict and confidently assert only one of the possible answers in the large majority of cases. Furthermore, there is a consistent bias across models in which languages are preferred, with a general bias against Russian and, for the longest context lengths, in favor of Chinese. Both of these patterns are consistent between models trained inside and outside of mainland China, though somewhat stronger in the former category.
2604.07123.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）已被證明在整合衝突信息以回答問題的過程中存在偏見。<lb/>在這裡，我們詢問這種偏見是否也存在於每個衝突信息所使用的語言方面。<lb/>為了回答這個問題，我們將衝突的針對性針對多語言環境進行擴展，並針對五種不同語言的自然新聞領域數據，對不同大小的多語言LLMs進行全面的評估。<lb/>我們發現所有測試的LLMs，包括GPT-5.2，在大多數情況下都忽略了衝突，自信地僅聲稱其中一個可能的答案。<lb/>此外，模型之間在語言偏好上存在一致的偏見，對俄語普遍存在偏見，而在最長的上下文長度中則偏向中文。<lb/>這兩種模式在中國大陸內外訓練的模型之間是一致的，雖然在前者類別中稍微強一些。
2604.07123.author: Robert Östling et.al.
